summary: Decompiled four gmmenu OnLoad routines and tightened related game-mode headers/types. The PR replaced placeholder declarations for gm_801A50B8_OnLoad, gm_801A5130_OnLoad, gm_801A51A8_OnLoad, and gm_801A5220_OnLoad with typed void(void) prototypes, implemented their menu state setup logic, and introduced a minimal struct UnkAllstarData so global gm_80473A18 could be typed enough to access its byte buffer.
what_changed: Implemented gm_801A50B8_OnLoad using data returned by gm_8017EB30, forwarding selected byte fields to gm_801BEFA4, gm_801BEFC0, gm_801BF000, gm_801BEFE0, and gm_801BF020(1).
Implemented gm_801A5130_OnLoad with the same byte-field setup pattern but using gm_8017E424 and passing gm_801BF020(0).
Implemented gm_801A51A8_OnLoad using global gm_80473A18.pad_x0 as the source byte array and passing gm_801BF020(2).
Implemented gm_801A5220_OnLoad as a small scene transition wrapper calling gm_SetSceneMinor(gm_801BF718()).
Changed gmmenu.h declarations from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholders to concrete void function(void) prototypes for the four OnLoad functions.
Added struct UnkAllstarData with s8 pad_x0[0xA0] in gm/types.h.
Changed gm_80473A18 declaration in gm_1601.h from extern UNK_T to extern struct UnkAllstarData.
Added includes in gmmenu.c for dolphin/types.h and gm subsystem headers needed by the new implementations.
smart_moves: Typed gm_80473A18 only as far as the current matching work required: a named struct with a padded byte array. This avoids overclaiming unknown field semantics while enabling direct indexed accesses needed by the decompiled code.

Recognized and reused the same branch pattern in three OnLoad routines: if the first byte is 0x12 and byte 0x12 is nonzero, promote the value sent to gm_801BEFA4 to 0x13; otherwise use the original first byte.
Kept source bytes as u8/u8* temporaries in gmmenu.c, which matches the byte-indexed nature of the original data and avoids unnecessary struct field invention.
Upgraded prototypes at the same time as implementations, reducing placeholder leakage and making call signatures explicit.
decomp_lessons: For unknown game-mode data blobs, a temporary padded struct can be preferable to UNK_T when global data needs repeated byte indexing across a file.

When several nearby OnLoad routines share a state-copy pattern, matching one can guide the others: identify the data source, keep the same byte offsets, and vary only the final mode selector argument.
Do not invent named fields prematurely for offsets like 0x0, 0x1, 0x3, 0x4, and 0x12 when only raw byte behavior is evidenced; a pad_x0 byte array documents uncertainty while preserving access shape.
Replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with void(void) is an important cleanup once a function is implemented and no arguments/return are observed.
Small scene wrappers may match cleanly by preserving nested call form, as shown by gm_SetSceneMinor(gm_801BF718()).
naming_conventions: Kept address-based symbol names for still-unknown functions: gm_801A50B8_OnLoad, gm_801A5130_OnLoad, gm_801A51A8_OnLoad, gm_801A5220_OnLoad, gm_801BF718.
Used OnLoad suffix for game-mode load callbacks.
Named the minimally understood global-data type struct UnkAllstarData, indicating probable All-Star relation while preserving uncertainty with the Unk prefix.
Used pad_x0 for an unknown byte buffer starting at offset 0, consistent with decomp placeholder field naming.
Used lowercase local compiler-style names var_r0 and temp_r3 in matched routines, reflecting register-derived decomp naming rather than semantic invention.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Explicit casts such as (s32) temp_r3[0] and (u8) temp_r3[0x12] were used in branch conditions, likely to preserve signedness/extension behavior for matching.
The code masks var_r0 with 0xFF when passing to gm_801BEFA4, preserving byte-width behavior after promotion to a wider argument type.

The three setup functions preserve byte-index offsets exactly: 0x0 controls the primary menu value, 0x12 gates the 0x12-to-0x13 conversion, and offsets 0x1, 0x3, and 0x4 are forwarded to separate gm_801BEFxx/gm_801BF000 setters.

The two pointer-source versions differ only in their source accessor, gm_8017EB30 versus gm_8017E424, and final gm_801BF020 selector value, a useful pattern for matching duplicated menu initialization code.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace struct UnkAllstarData.pad_x0 with named fields or expand its layout beyond 0xA0 bytes.
Search for additional gmmenu OnLoad functions using the same gm_801BEFA4/gm_801BEFC0/gm_801BF000/gm_801BEFE0/gm_801BF020 setup sequence.
Check whether gm_8017EB30 and gm_8017E424 later receive typed return structs instead of u8* usage.
Look for later renames of gm_801BEFA4, gm_801BEFC0, gm_801BF000, gm_801BEFE0, gm_801BF020, or gm_801BF718 that clarify the menu state being configured.
